name: Founder Pulse
description: WF-60 growth-funnel overview for founder/on-call. Aggregates DAU/WAU/MAU, signup→onboarding→first_action activation funnel (overall + per module), MRR / new-subscriptions, D1/D7/D30 retention and activation rate from the canonical events declared in packages/shared/src/lib/analyticsEvents.ts. Runbook: docs/observability/posthog-founder-pulse.md.

first_action_completed.idempotency: hub_first_action_completed_v1:<module> KV flag (once per module per account)

panels.name: Active users — DAU / WAU / MAU
panels.description: Rolling unique-user counts over yesterday-anchored 1d / 7d / 30d windows. Cohort is `any event in window`, not just `$pageview`, so backend-only events (Stripe webhook → `subscription_started`) still count the user as active.
panels.rationale: DAU/WAU/MAU is the load-bearing health signal for the product. WAU/DAU ratio (stickiness) and MAU month-over-month trajectory live downstream of this panel.

targets.MAU MoM growth: ≥5% / month (P2 below; P1 if negative two months in a row)

query.query: SELECT 'DAU' AS metric, uniq(distinct_id) AS users FROM events WHERE timestamp >= toStartOfDay(now() - INTERVAL 1 DAY) AND timestamp < toStartOfDay(now()) UNION ALL SELECT 'WAU' AS metric, uniq(distinct_id) AS users FROM events WHERE timestamp >= toStartOfDay(now() - INTERVAL 7 DAY) AND timestamp < toStartOfDay(now()) UNION ALL SELECT 'MAU' AS metric, uniq(distinct_id) AS users FROM events WHERE timestamp >= toStartOfDay(now() - INTERVAL 30 DAY) AND timestamp < toStartOfDay(now())

breakdown.comment: Web vs ios vs android via the platform super-property registered in apps/web/src/core/observability/posthog.ts.

panels.name: WF-60 activation funnel — signup → onboarding → first_action
panels.description: Strict-sequence funnel over the four canonical activation milestones. Conversion window: 7 days from signup. Time range: last 28 days. Use to spot WoW regressions in any single step.
panels.rationale: PR-06/07/08/09 wire these events. The funnel here is the single chart that compresses 'are new sign-ups becoming activated users?' into one number. Cross-references `60-growth-funnel-snapshot.json` n8n cron which snapshots the same four steps daily.

breakdown.comment: Person-property `vibe` is an array of module picks. PostHog flattens — each user contributes once per pick. Off-diagonal funnel mass spots vibe-blind onboarding.

panels.name: WF-60 activation funnel per module
panels.description: Same funnel as `wf60-funnel-overall` but breaks down step 3 (`first_action_completed`) by the event-payload `module` field. Returns four funnels — one per Hub module (finyk, fizruk, routine, nutrition).
panels.rationale: Audit failure mode: a user picks `finyk` in onboarding but the first real action is logged in `nutrition`. Module-bucketed funnel quantifies that misalignment per module so module-specific FTUX fixes (PR-08 `markFirstActionCompletedForModule`) can be triaged on data, not guesses.

targets.per-module signup → first_action: Each of finyk/fizruk/routine/nutrition ≥40% within 7 days (P2 below)
targets.module skew: If any single module captures >70% of first_actions and another <10%, the onboarding recommendation engine is leaking — file Linear ticket.

panels.name: Activation rate (D1)
panels.description: Share of users who completed any `first_action_completed` event within 24h of `signup_completed`. Computed daily for the last 28 days and plotted as a line — the curve is the canary for onboarding regressions.
panels.rationale: Activation rate compresses the funnel into a single percentage. Crossing the D1 boundary is the textbook signal that the FTUX promise was kept on session-1. Below, the AARRR funnel collapses regardless of upstream signup volume.

targets.regression alert: drops ≥10pp WoW → P1 (page on-call; likely deploy regression in `first_action_completed` instrumentation or KV-flag namespace drift)

query.query: WITH signups AS (SELECT distinct_id, min(timestamp) AS signed_at FROM events WHERE event = 'signup_completed' AND timestamp >= now() - INTERVAL 28 DAY GROUP BY distinct_id), first_actions AS (SELECT distinct_id, min(timestamp) AS acted_at FROM events WHERE event = 'first_action_completed' GROUP BY distinct_id) SELECT toDate(s.signed_at) AS cohort_day, count() AS signups, countIf(fa.acted_at IS NOT NULL AND fa.acted_at <= s.signed_at + INTERVAL 1 DAY) AS activated, round(activated * 1.0 / signups, 4) AS d1_activation_rate FROM signups s LEFT JOIN first_actions fa ON s.distinct_id = fa.distinct_id GROUP BY cohort_day ORDER BY cohort_day

panels.name: New subscriptions — count + new MRR
panels.description: Daily count of `subscription_started` events plus the new-MRR contribution (monthly-equivalent revenue) split by cadence. PostHog `$revenue` super-property is set in `apps/server/src/modules/billing/stripe.ts` and stores the major-unit amount.
panels.rationale: Until a renewals/cancellations feed is wired into PostHog (TBD), the new-MRR contribution per day is the cleanest revenue-pulse signal. Yearly subs are normalised by /12 so monthly + yearly stack on the same axis. Active-MRR (cumulative) is computed downstream in n8n (WF-60) — this panel is the per-day delta.

targets.new-MRR run-rate: ≥$50/month aggregated over trailing 7 days (P2 below; baseline tracked in docs/launch/business/01-monetization-and-pricing.md)
targets.subscription_started ZEROES: If new subs = 0 over 7 days while ≥10 signups/day → Stripe webhook regression. P1.

query.query: SELECT toDate(timestamp) AS day, properties.cadence AS cadence, count() AS new_subs, sum(toFloat(properties.$revenue) * if(properties.cadence = 'yearly', 1.0/12, 1.0)) AS new_mrr_usd FROM events WHERE event = 'subscription_started' AND timestamp >= now() - INTERVAL 28 DAY AND properties.status IN ('active', 'trialing') GROUP BY day, cadence ORDER BY day, cadence

panels.name: Cohort retention — D1 / D7 / D30
panels.description: Retention from `signup_completed` cohort. Returning event = any event (matches DAU contract). Cohort granularity = day, period = 30 days back, 30 days forward.
panels.rationale: Three retention floors gate the entire growth thesis: D1 (came back at all), D7 (FTUX promise sustained beyond curiosity), D30 (subscription-conversion window). Together they describe the shape of the leaky bucket — same metric the AARRR canon and Reichheld retention math use.

targets.D30: ≥8% (baseline TBD post-billing; update with first 90 days of data)

breakdown.comment: Same vibe segmentation as funnel-overall — keeps retention readable per onboarding intent.

panels.name: Funnel ZEROES — per-step daily count
panels.description: Daily count of each canonical funnel event over the last 14 days. Single-tile early-warning for `count() = 0` regressions on any of the four steps.
panels.rationale: Every previous PostHog outage where canonical events stopped firing took 3–9 days to detect through funnel-shape alone (per posthog-ftux-dashboards.md §5 'Funnel ZEROES'). A raw daily-count tile is the cheapest leading indicator — if any cell flips to 0 unexpectedly, page on-call.

targets.any step = 0 / 24h while previous 7d > 0: P1 — deploy broke `trackEvent` calls or KV namespace drifted.

query.query: SELECT toDate(timestamp) AS day, countIf(event = 'signup_completed') AS signups, countIf(event = 'onboarding_completed') AS onboardings, countIf(event = 'first_action_completed') AS first_actions, countIf(event = 'subscription_started') AS subscriptions FROM events WHERE timestamp >= now() - INTERVAL 14 DAY GROUP BY day ORDER BY day

alerts.condition: any one of signups / onboardings / first_actions / subscriptions = 0 over 24h while the previous 7-day window had > 0
sum(new_subs) = 0 over 7 rolling days while signups/day average > 10
D1 retention drops > 5pp from the 28-day baseline for any signup-cohort
